taxonomy: Chytraculia axillaris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Chytraculia, which is a group of plants native to Southern Africa. Within the Fabaceae family, Chytraculia axillaris is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. This classification suggests that the plant is a type of legume, known for its fruit pods and nitrogen-fixing abilities. As part of the Fabaceae family, Chytraculia axillaris is closely related to other plants like beans, peas, and lentils.
